authoritative_sources.title: Final Rule on Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA traceability rule is the primary U.S. public baseline for lot-level critical tracking events and key data elements used to validate CPG evidence before analysis or disclosure.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory source for records, holding, release, distribution, and supply-chain program boundaries in food CPG operations.
used_for: where analytics stops and formal quality or supplier-program ownership begins

authoritative_sources.title: A Food Labeling Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's food labeling guide is the authoritative public reference for disclosure and product-claim framing that constrains consumer-facing language derived from this lane's analysis.

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Nutrition Labeling Manual: A Guide for Developing and Using Data Bases
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's nutrition labeling manual is an authoritative metric-definition and evidence-quality reference for database development and analytical assumptions behind product-attribute reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: Health Products Compliance Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC guidance is the primary U.S. public source for substantiation expectations around health-related claims, which constrains unsupported causal or consumer-facing claims from operational data.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for cross-enterprise traceability and identifier exchanges used broadly across CPG supply networks.

authoritative_sources.title: GTIN Management Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 general specifications are the recognized public baseline for GTIN, packaging hierarchy, and product-identification semantics needed to reconcile CPG item evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code: Food Manufacturing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQF is a recognized operating reference used across CPG manufacturing to define nonconforming product, release, supplier, and records expectations beyond minimum regulation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Escalation for quality holds, supplier nonconformance, or release decisions

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect product, batch, lot, inventory, supplier, quality, and traceability records and identify the authoritative truth set before analysis begins.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Metric calculation or report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Define metric logic, denominators, source hierarchy, refresh assumptions, and report grain for CPG analytical work.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis or disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Translate calculated outputs into analytical narratives, caveats, and disclosure-aware summaries without drifting into unsupported claims or approvals.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data
workflow_stages.description: Stop or downgrade outputs when source extracts are stale, denominator logic is disputed, or systems disagree on the outcome.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Traceability-aware execution or analysis
workflow_stages.description: Preserve batch, lot, inventory, and release lineage in every analysis and refuse to treat unresolved traceability conflicts as ordinary noise.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for quality holds, supplier nonconformance, or release decisions
workflow_stages.description: Route hold, release, nonconformance, substitution, and supplier-approval cases to adjacent specialists with the evidence package intact.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: quality holds, release records, and nonconformance reports
metric definitions, caveat notes, and analytical briefs

allowed: Define metrics, source hierarchy, refresh assumptions, and caveat posture for CPG consumer-insights requests.
Produce evidence-backed analytical briefs, reporting packages, and reconciliation notes grounded in batch, lot, inventory, supplier, and quality records.
Normalize CPG terminology and preserve source lineage without implying unsupported approval or release authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, source precedence, facility context, or product-category rules materially change the metric outcome.
Quality hold, release, nonconformance, engineering, or supplier-approval issues change whether the analysis can safely proceed.
Different systems produce unreconciled outcomes or conflicting policy posture for the same reporting period or disclosure context.
refuse: Requests for quality release, supplier approval, engineering disposition, legal interpretation, or final consumer-facing claim approval.
Requests for stronger causal, trend, benchmark, or disclosure claims than the records support.
Publication or reporting requests where records are incomplete, stale, or conflicting enough to change the conclusion.

domain_failure_modes: Using a consumer metric denominator that mixes shipped, delivered, held, and saleable inventory states without explicit logic.
Treating stale inventory or quality extracts as current and publishing unsupported trend conclusions.
Collapsing ERP, MES, WMS, supplier, and QMS conflicts into a single metric without a reconciliation note.
Treating an open quality hold or release boundary as a minor caveat instead of a stop condition that changes the reporting posture.
Assuming supplier substitution equivalence or rework acceptance without formal approval.
Crossing from descriptive analytics into unsupported causal, policy, or consumer-facing disclosure claims.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline
Low-confidence handling when required facts or records are missing
